root_text.sn23.11:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā rādho bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn23.11:1.5: “Rūpaṁ kho, rādha, māro, vedanā māro, saññā māro, saṅkhārā māro, viññāṇaṁ māro.
root_text.sn23.11:1.6: Evaṁ passaṁ, rādha, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpasmimpi nibbindati, vedanāyapi nibbindati, saññāyapi nibbindati, saṅkhāresupi nibbindati, viññāṇasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn23.11:1.7: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati. Vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn23.11:1.8: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn23.11:1.2: Seated to one side, Venerable Rādha said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn23.11:1.3: “Sir, they speak of this thing called ‘Māra’.
translation_text.sn23.11:1.5: “Rādha, form is Māra, feeling is Māra, perception is Māra, choices are Māra, consciousness is Māra.
translation_text.sn23.11:1.6: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn23.11:1.7: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn23.11:1.8: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”

variant_text.sn23.11:0.2: Dutiyamāravagga → dutiyavaggo (sya-all); dutiyo vaggo (pts1ed); dutiyavagga (csp1ed, csp2ed)
